chat_template_prefix_alignment.controller_only_feasibility: A raw Task-A request can be constructed from the same rendered assistant-message surface used by future Task-B prompts while retaining strict grammar-constrained JSON capture, but exact tokenizer parity and captured generated-token equality must be proved before downstream contact.

expected_effect.full_json_prefix_extension: conditional on exact equality between captured Task-A generated token IDs and the corresponding Task-B lexical-prefix token IDs
expected_effect.static_guarantee: The four-token disabled-thinking scaffold is removed. Rendered-text alignment alone does not guarantee a longer executable boundary, but an authenticated exact token-array strict extension can preserve the complete retained terminal state when the bound source predicate bypasses checkpoint restoration.

fairness_requirements: preserve identical Task-A evidence, question, answer choices, seed, structured schema, and output ceiling
preserve exact captured Task-A JSON and downstream Task-B semantic information
score Task-A and downstream utility because removing four model-visible tokens may change behavior
prove the controller-only request surface changes no protected scoring input
chat_template_prefix_alignment.reason_not_selected: Alignment can remove the scaffold and may expose a longer matching JSON token history, but rendered text alone does not prove exact token identity. The lower-scope exact-token extension route must authenticate emitted token IDs, omit only the terminal EOG that was not decoded into retained state, and satisfy the complete source predicate. It can establish longer live inheritance, not restoration.

no_same_panel_response.reason: The authenticated four-pair result already establishes partial inherited executable reuse, equal Task-B accuracy, lower measured fresh-token cost, and the exact causal boundary. Repeating it only under a renamed gate would add no new task or mechanism evidence.

full_source_law: R = exact old/new prompt-token longest common prefix
pos_next = prior prompt token position at R
if retained-state pos_min < pos_min_threshold, preserve the live state and predict R admitted tokens
otherwise search the saved checkpoint inventory
select the newest saved checkpoint with pos_max <= pos_next and (pos_min < pos_min_threshold or pos_min == 0)
if no eligible checkpoint exists, predict zero admitted tokens
if the new request exactly equals the retained prompt, apply the source one-token prompt-logits correction
outcome_independent_inputs: context-checkpoint count, minimum spacing, and exact checkpoint inventory
exact old and future prompt token sequences
message spans and prior prompt schedule
same-slot cache-enabled request order with no intervening slot mutation
derivation_law.precontact_binding: Freeze the derivation algorithm and every runtime/source input before the experiment. For a model-authored carrier, commit the exact numeric boundary after authenticating Task A and before contacting the model for any downstream branch; never derive it from observed cached-token telemetry.
derivation_law.telemetry_gate: observed admitted cached_prompt_tokens must equal the predicted safe executable count

runtime_native_executable_carrier_bounda.feasibility: The imported server deterministically computes the raw token common prefix, compares retained-state position with a source-defined n_swa threshold, preserves the live state when no rollback is needed, otherwise restores the latest eligible saved checkpoint because arbitrary DSV4 partial rollback is unavailable, and reports the resulting n_past as cached tokens. With source, runtime, prompt geometry, checkpoint provenance, and schedule bound, the admitted executable boundary is predictable before each downstream request.
limitations: the current decision law is not changed retroactively
not all Task-A generated tokens are claimed inherited
a source or runtime-configuration change requires a new derivation and qualification

checkpoint_eligibility_or_offset_change.expected_effect: May make a later checkpoint eligible and remove some or all rollback.
checkpoint_eligibility_or_offset_change.risk: Changes server checkpoint semantics and runtime identity; incorrect state coverage can silently corrupt hybrid recurrent execution.
checkpoint_eligibility_or_offset_change.system_configuration: Requires a rebuilt binary and newly bound checkpoint configuration.

exact_token_array_strict_extension.expected_effect: For the bound model, whose GGUF has no sliding-window metadata and therefore uses source-default n_swa zero, a future request whose exact token array strictly extends every retained terminal token has pos_min below pos_next and can bypass checkpoint restoration, admitting the full retained prefix.
exact_token_array_strict_extension.risk: Requires authenticated emitted token IDs, exact BOS and message-boundary handling, omission only of an authenticated terminal EOG that was returned but not decoded into retained state, and fail-closed proof that the future input begins with the complete retained token vector. It is live inheritance only and provides no root restoration.
exact_token_array_strict_extension.system_configuration: No system-level change; preserve one sidecar epoch, cache_prompt true, return_tokens true, exact binary/model/runtime identity, and the frozen cache configuration.

slot_snapshot_restore.expected_effect: The existing full sequence-state serializer includes DSV4 raw state, compressed caches, and compressor state and can preserve a terminal root independently of partial checkpoints. Restoring into a fresh slot is the smallest source-present route to test branch isolation or closure.
slot_snapshot_restore.risk: Historical file-specific attribution was inconclusive in process and restart persistence failed; the restored slot lacks the original in-memory checkpoint list. Exact continuation therefore needs an exact-token extension or a newly proven checkpoint policy, and full byte-exact restoration remains unexecuted.
slot_snapshot_restore.system_configuration: Enable the existing slot-save path and use a fresh slot, likely with parallel capacity of at least two; bind the snapshot hash, binary, model, memory implementation, and runtime parameters.

smaller_n_ubatch.expected_effect: Reduces but does not eliminate the rollback while changing runtime configuration.
smaller_n_ubatch.risk: May reduce prompt-processing throughput and still optimizes the metric rather than closing restoration.
smaller_n_ubatch.system_configuration: Change n_ubatch and rebind the runtime identity.

source_sequence_copy.expected_effect: Could support process-local root branching if compressed and recurrent state were copied exactly.
source_sequence_copy.risk: The current DSV4 seq_cp copies raw cache state and clears compressed state, so it is not an exact terminal-root mechanism.
source_sequence_copy.system_configuration: Requires a rebuilt binary and additional sequence or slot capacity.

terminal_generation_checkpoint.expected_effect: A checkpoint captured at an authenticated generation boundary could target exact terminal executable-state inheritance and later restoration.
terminal_generation_checkpoint.risk: Requires new lifecycle, state-coverage, branch-isolation, and performance validation for hybrid recurrent memory.
terminal_generation_checkpoint.system_configuration: Requires a rebuilt binary and explicit root-count and memory ceilings.

terminal_safe_checkpoint_or_rollback_rep.selection_reason: The current runtime already delivered equal accuracy with a measured 43.74490627546862 percent fresh-token reduction, and exact-token strict extension is a lower-scope source-supported route to test full live inheritance. Engine repair is justified later only when exact restoration remains the explicit causal objective after existing full-state snapshot surfaces are tested.

mechanisms.lesson: A reusable carrier must be actively addressed and identity-stamped; passive tape structure alone does not accelerate computation.
mechanisms.relevance: Supports binding each executable carrier to exact prompt, runtime, and task identity before reuse.

mechanisms.lesson: Fingerprint-checked multi-tree cache reuse and graceful fallback are the closest CAT_CAS analogues to branching from one authenticated carrier.
mechanisms.relevance: Supports the selected multi-branch next axis, not a same-panel rerun.

mechanisms.lesson: Warm-tape hash-addressed slots illustrate reusable rooted state but are standalone synthetic/Rust mechanisms, not a drop-in repair for llama-server DSV4 checkpoints.
mechanisms.relevance: Informs future carrier identity and branch accounting without changing the minimum current response.

cat_cas_assessment.repository_claim_caution: CAT_CAS README and report claims are navigation and hypothesis sources; they do not override Neo3000 authenticated runtime evidence or establish a production server mechanism.

confirmed_boundary.template_boundary: four-token disabled-thinking scaffold versus captured assistant JSON

minimum_future_properties.admitted_cache_gate: observed admitted cache equals the preregistered source-predicted executable boundary
boundary_binding_inputs: model, memory type, and model-derived n_swa
checkpoint count, spacing, inventory, ordinal, token count, pos_min, and pos_max
exact carrier and branch token hashes, raw common-prefix hash, and token geometry
authenticated emitted-token array and terminal-stop identity when strict extension is used
message spans, prior prompt schedule, and no intervening slot mutation
minimum_future_properties.new_scientific_value_required: held-out task family, held-out transformation, multiple related branches, or another nonduplicative catalytic axis

rejected_responses.reason: Rendered-text alignment can remove the four-token scaffold but does not by itself establish exact token identity, terminal-state inheritance, or restoration. Exact token-array strict extension is retained as an adjunct under the selected runtime-native boundary.
An engine or runtime intervention is disproportionate while the existing runtime already demonstrates equal-accuracy lower fresh-token cost; reserve it for an explicit restoration experiment.
Repeating the completed four pairs under a prospective carrier definition adds no new evidence and would risk retroactive metric optimization.

selected_next_scientific_axis.reason: Multiple held-out related branches from one authenticated executable carrier add new evidence about recursively reusable state and amortization while exercising the already measured boundary honestly. Preparation should first combine authenticated exact-token strict extension with the existing process-local RAM or full-state snapshot surfaces needed to isolate branches. This is more catalytic and less duplicative than repeating the same four pairs or spending an engine change solely to improve lexical exactness.
selected_next_scientific_axis.scope_ceiling: Static preparation only in the next operation; no successor design or execution is included here.

selection_reason: The bound server and DSV4 sources make the safe executable checkpoint predictable from exact token geometry and runtime identity, and the existing panel already shows equal accuracy with lower measured fresh-token cost. The minimum useful response is to use that runtime-native boundary prospectively, retire the same-panel line, and add new multi-branch evidence before pursuing an engine-level restoration mechanism.

source_feasibility_evidence.finding: The server obtains n_past from the exact old/new token common prefix, preserves live state when retained pos_min is below the n_swa threshold, otherwise restores the latest eligible context checkpoint when partial removal is unavailable, then reports resulting n_past as cached tokens.
Under exact strict extension with the bound n_swa zero, DSV4 reports retained pos_min as the prior final position, which is below pos_next; the checkpoint-restoration predicate is therefore false.
The exact bound GGUF has no sliding-window metadata, the Qwen3.5-MoE loader does not override n_swa, and the model hparams default is zero.
The source model-hparams default defines n_swa zero as no sliding-window attention.
Prompt common-prefix identity is exact token equality over the prior and requested server token sequences.
The common runtime probes memory partial-removal capability and routes full-only memories through checkpoint restoration.
DSV4 rejects arbitrary p0 greater than zero because compressed running state is not reconstructible from raw cache; sequence copy clears compressed state.

Full sequence-state serialization includes the DSV4 raw cache, compressed caches, and compressor states. Existing slot save and restore call that full serializer, making a fresh-slot snapshot a source-present but scientifically unproven branch-root route.

The server returns sampled token IDs when requested, but an EOG sample stops generation before that token is decoded into retained slot state. Exact strict-extension construction must bind the captured token events and omit only that terminal EOG.
The exact sidecar launch binds batch 512, microbatch 128, eight context checkpoints, and minimum checkpoint spacing 512 for the published runtime geometry.
The raw completion surface can preserve strict JSON-schema output constraints for a future controller-only chat-alignment adjunct.
The inherited-carrier controller binds exact rendered prompt and token geometry before each downstream request and records observed reuse separately.
Zero-contact replay reconstructs the four-token template divergence, 128-token checkpoint rollback, and exact per-pair arithmetic from authenticated evidence.
